content: Interstate 19 in Arizona is the only interstate highway in the United States that uses kilometer posts instead of mileposts. This is because I-19 was originally constructed as a state highway in the 1960s, and at that time, Arizona was using the metric system for highway signage. As a result, I-19 retained its kilometer posts when it was upgraded to an interstate highway in 1978.
